rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenylation-dearomative ipso-cyclization of N-benzylacrylamides with diaryl diselenides to access selenylated 2-azaspiro[4.5]decadienones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) generates a PhSe radical and a selenium cation; cathode reduces the cyclohexadienyl radical cation intermediate to the corresponding carbanion that undergoes demethylation to deliver the spirocyclic dienone

key_intermediates.name: phenylselenyl radical (PhSe•) from anodic oxidation of (PhSe)2
C-centered radical C from PhSe• addition to acrylamide alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical D after ipso-carbocyclization onto the p-methoxyaryl
cyclohexadienyl cation E after SET at cathode (then demethylation)

electron_flow: At the anode, (PhSe)2 is oxidized to a radical cation that fragments to PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to the acrylamide alkene to give a C-radical that undergoes ipso-cyclization onto the p-methoxyphenyl ring to form a cyclohexadienyl radical, which is oxidized (at the anode) / reduced at the cathode pathway then loses methyl to furnish the dearomatized spirocyclic dienone.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Formation of spirocycle 3a was inhibited and a TEMPO-SePh adduct 6 was detected by HRMS, supporting PhSe radical intermediacy

probe_reagents.name: No current (electrolysis off)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formation in the absence of current, confirming that electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak of (PhSe)2 (2a) at 1.18 V vs 1.72 V for acrylamide 1a, indicating diselenide is oxidized preferentially at the anode

notes: Reaction fails with 4-NO2 substituent on the N-benzyl aryl (electron-poor arene cannot be dearomatized) and with Me2Se2 (no selenyl radical generated). The cathodic step is drawn as SET to convert dienyl radical D to cation/anion E followed by demethylation; the exact polarity of E (cation after anodic oxidation vs anion after cathodic reduction) is ambiguous in the scheme, but the authors invoke cathodic SET.
